hierarchical_target: [L1] Abstract Solid Assembly [L2] An assembly of three distinct topological bodies bounded by B-spline surfaces and advanced faces. [L3] Bounding box approximately derived from point coordinates: maximum Z = 425.38 mm and maximum X = 305.12 mm.

[L1] Telescope Hinge and Upper Band Assembly [L2] A mechanical hinge assembly comprising an upper band and a main hinge body, featuring multiple cylindrical and conical surfaces with toroidal fillets. [L3] The assembly features a main cylindrical surface with a radius of 82.07 mm and smaller cylindrical bores with a radius of 3.175 mm. It also contains conical chamfers with radii of 60.93 mm and 62.6 mm. The bounding dimensions include a point extending to Y = 12.7 mm.

[L1] Headphone Speaker Component [L2] A closed shell manifold solid representing a headphone speaker, bounded by multiple cylindrical, conical, toroidal, and spherical surfaces interconnected by complex edges. [L3] The geometry features primary cylindrical surfaces with radii of 40.07 mm and 28.87 mm, alongside a large spherical surface with a radius of 135.39 mm. Bounding box coordinates indicate extents reaching an absolute magnitude of X = 88.2 mm, Y = 32.2 mm, and a Z-axis depth (incorporating the spherical center origin) of -135.39 mm.

[L1] Pivot Hinge [L2] A pivot hinge mechanism comprised of a main manifold solid bounded by several cylindrical holes and planar faces. [L3] Features cylindrical pins or holes with radii of 5.715 mm and 3.175 mm. Bounding box coordinates include points up to X = 8.1 mm, Y = 2.26 mm, Z = 6.07 mm.

[L1] Headphone Hinge [L2] A headphone hinge component featuring a primary cylindrical housing and smaller connecting cylindrical pins, surrounded by complex B-spline surfaces. [L3] Main cylindrical housing with a radius of 22.71 mm. Features multiple small cylindrical through-holes or pins with a radius of 1.27 mm. Overall bounding box length extends to at least X = 30.48 mm, Z = 3.048 mm.

[L1] Multi-part Assembly [L2] A complex assembly comprising 10 distinct manifold solid parts (Part 1 to Part 10), consisting of numerous planar faces and interconnected topological shells. [L3] The assembly defines a relatively large bounding box with a maximum known Cartesian point extending to X = 365.76 mm.

[L1] Symmetrical Base Component [L2] A closed shape representation composed of advanced faces and directed boundary edges. [L3] The component's bounding dimensions track close to the origin, extending minimally along the Z-axis to approximately 1.58 mm.

[L1] Circular Profile Geometry [L2] A styled geometric representation featuring circular edge loops bounding defined surface areas. [L3] The geometry's cartesian definitions show a primary bounding range anchoring at X = 91.04 mm and Z = 25.4 mm.

[L1] Precision Machined Element [L2] A solid CAD object characterized by angular directional constraints and Cartesian alignments. [L3] Spatial extents extracted from the topology record bounded features sitting at an elevation of Z = 14.80 mm relative to the local origin.

[L1] Recessed Structural Feature [L2] A topological shape defined by directed planes intersecting negative coordinates to form a bounded solid. [L3] The geometry extends into the negative Cartesian space, with bounding vertices located at a depth of Z = -52.10 mm.

[L1] Elongated Mechanical Component [L2] A solid body characterized by interconnected surfaces forming a topological shell. [L3] The component defines a bounding box with an extreme X-axis span including coordinates up to 227.6 mm and -43.26 mm.

[L1] Machined Block Component [L2] A bounded solid manifold characterized by various planar intersections and topological bounding loops. [L3] The object's bounding box coordinates indicate a span extending approximately from X = -58.24 mm to X = 54.26 mm.

[L1] Structural Component [L2] A topological body composed of complex face boundaries connecting sections. [L3] The parametric specification derived from Cartesian points shows bounding extents including X = -40.45 mm and X = 49.28 mm, with a Z extent reaching 4.62 mm.

[L1] Precision Fitting [L2] A connected closed shell represented by advanced faces, featuring various cartesian point delimitations for edges. [L3] Based on boundary values, the part has local extremes at X = 49.72 mm and Z = 43.83 mm.

[L1] Internal Core Component [L2] A topological solid forming an advanced shape representation bounded by surface styling and face limits. [L3] The geometry defines spatial boundaries reaching an approximate minimum X of -49.77 mm and a maximum X of 48.32 mm.
[L1] Complex Mechanical Housing [L2] A topological solid forming an advanced shape representation bounded by surface styling. [L3] The geometry defines spatial boundaries anchored near the origin.

[L1] Internal Core Component [L2] A bounded manifold solid characterized by orthogonal directional constraints. [L3] Spatial extents extracted from the topology record features sitting exactly at the global origin.

[L1] Extruded Profile Geometry [L2] A connected closed shell defined by linear directional vectors and planar boundaries. [L3] The shape incorporates normalized directional vectors spanning the primary axes.

[L1] Symmetrical Angled Element [L2] A geometric solid structured with mirrored angular projections and boundary limits. [L3] Directional vectors indicate mirrored negative and positive angular constraints along the X-Z plane.

Bounding box dimensions derived from point coordinates extend from X = -0.5 mm to X = 10.5 mm, Y = -20.43 mm to Y = 23.42 mm, and a minimum Z point of -55.16 mm.

[L1] Elongated Base Mount [L2] An interconnected manifold solid featuring aligned orthogonal normals and defined boundary constraints. [L3] Extracted geometry parameters record a significant projection along the Y-axis to 209.85 mm.

[L1] Cylindrical Shaft Assembly [L2] A topological solid forming an advanced shape representation with linear vector extrusions. [L3] The geometry lies flush on the Y-Z plane relative to the origin, extending out to X = 238.60 mm.
